description: You are a high school physics teacher at an OLPC-equipped school preparing a worksheet on optics. Write a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/snells_law.py that calculates the angle of refraction using Snell's Law and generates a Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) diagram of the light path. The script must calculate the angle for a light ray traveling from Air (n1 = 1.00) into Crown Glass (n2 = 1.52) with an incident angle of 45 degrees. The generated SVG, saved to /home/ga/Documents/refraction.svg, must include graphical elements for the boundary interface, the normal line, the incident ray, and the refracted ray. It must also include text labels for the mediums ('Air' and 'Glass'), the incident angle ('45'), and the calculated refracted angle rounded to at least one decimal place.
